rulings.Case Title: JASON SELLO VS. COMPASS CALIFORNIA II, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE Amended CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 7, 2023 line 1. CROSS DEFENDANT SOTHEBY'S INTERNATIONAL REALTY, INC., MARY LOU CASTELLANOS MOTION TO STRIKE Amended CROSS COMPLAINT is DENIED in part and moot in part. The motion to strike causes action asserted against the moving defendant for the first time in the Second Amended Cross-Complaint is DENIED. The Court will address these causes of action on the merits on the demurrer. The motion is otherwise moot in light of the ruling on the demurrer. =(501/HEK) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JASON SELLO VS. COMPASS CALIFORNIA II, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 7, 2023 line 1. CROSS DEFENDANT SOTHEBY'S INTERNATIONAL REALTY, INC., MARY LOU CASTELLANOS DEMURRER to Amended CROSS COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with final leave to amend as to the causes of action currently alleged against the moving cross-defendant only. Plaintiff does not have leave to amend to include any new causes of action or add this defendant to any other causes of action currently asserted in the cross-complaint against other cross-defendants. Cross-Complainant shall have 30 days to (1) allege facts in support of negligence cause of action and recovery for negligent infliction of emotional distress; facts in support of each element of negligence must be alleged as well as factual basis for recovery of emotional distress; (2) if cross-complainant seeks recovery of emotional distress damages under other currently asserted causes of action against the moving cross-defendant, cross-complaint must clearly indicate within such cause of action that emotional distress damages are being sought and allege facts in support of such damages; (3) to allege facts in support of recovery of attorney's fees under the tort of another doctrine; Cross-Defendant must be mindful of the authorities cited by the cross-defendant in the moving papers as to the applicability and availability of the tort of another recovery and recovery of fees in joint tortfeasor cases; (4) to allege facts in support of allegations that the moving cross-defendant interfered with an existing contract between the Seller and the Listing Agent; a contractual term must be alleged (cross-complainant may attach the Listing Agreement and identify the specific provisions in the body of the Cross-Complaint).=(501/HEK) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
